commentary: Ten sessions this week versus eleven last week, so technically a quieter week on paper, but that Mosman Run doing 13.5km is up from 13.1km last Saturday, and your heart rate came down from 159 to 149 — faster legs, calmer engine, which is exactly the direction you want to be going. The pool work held steady with three solid Prince Alfred sessions at 2000m each, and then you finished the week wading around Balmoral in what I can only assume was a very small swimsuit, which counts as both exercise and community service. Three weights sessions, three pool sessions, a long run, and a cheeky open water dip — that's not a drop-off week, that's a week that knew what it was doing.
metrics_commentary: That HRV spike to 73 ms when your seven-day average is sitting at 52 ms is your body basically winking at you — something recovered hard this week and it shows. Nearly 6700 metres in the pool is genuinely filthy work, and a VO2 Max of 57.3 means your engine is running like something that belongs on a poster. The run-walk at 13.5 km across one session is the only thing giving me pause, not because it's bad, but because one session means you went in once and left, and honey, that's a little shy of what we both know you're capable of spreading across the week. More movement days, same big gay energy — that's all I'm asking.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, full stop, programme hit exactly — no excuses, no rescheduling, no skipping leg day because it's hard. The PRs this week are genuinely embarrassing in the best possible way: leg press up to 168 kilograms, seated cable row cracking 84, incline dumbbell press at 34.7, and hammer curls jumping from 16.8 to 19.6, which is not a small leap for a curl. Weekly volume came in at 20,124 kilograms against last week's 23,660, which is lower, but lower volume with multiple PRs usually means the sessions were harder and heavier rather than lazier, so that's not a gap so much as a signal that the weight on the bar is genuinely going up. Strength is trending in exactly one direction and it is the right one, and every kilogram added to that leg press is another kilogram of someone getting more and more undeniably, visibly, deliciously himself.
